mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units sold (HOUST) signals a strengthening demand for durable goods, which in turn drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising HOUST are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 97.5 signals a supply-side shock that reduces the marginal product of capital, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 97.5 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) above 8.5% signals a weakening labor market, which in turn drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising UNRATE are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 1.5% signals a monetary easing cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 1.5 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in real GDP (GDPC1) above 13550 signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, which in turn drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising GDPC1 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 95 signals a supply-side shock that reduces the marginal product of capital, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 95 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) above 8% signals a weakening labor market, which in turn drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising UNRATE are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in real GDP (GDPC1) above 13550 and a 2-month increase in housing units sold (HOUST) above 750 signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, which in turn drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising GDPC1 and HOUST are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
